paragraph: The single-family market in Saddle Brook is moving steadily, with 29 sales over the last six months at a median of $650,000. Sellers are seeing results, with about 62 percent of homes selling above asking and a sale-to-list ratio of 1.01, which means list prices are holding up. Median days on market sits at 22, indicating homes priced right are attracting attention without sitting around. The range is substantial, from $444,000 to $1,220,000, so condition and location matter more than any town-wide price ceiling. Multi-family properties are sparse at three sales with a median of $740,000, but they've moved faster and stronger, with two-thirds selling above asking and a median of 28 days on market. The condo and townhouse tier offers the entry point at $437,000 median, with eight sales moving briskly at a median of 18.5 days on market and 62.5 percent selling above asking. If you're weighing single-family against condo, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family or the lower price point and low-maintenance lifestyle of a condo.
